page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7se.6xlarge is a general-purpose virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 24 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at a clock speed of 2.9 GHz. The instance is configured with 96.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or GPU hardware is bundled with this instance type, and it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place this server in the average tier across most standard compute and memory workloads. The Intel Xeon processor includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration. In synthetic testing, the instance achieved a Geekbench single-core score of 1575.0 and a multi-core score of 12067.0, alongside a PassMark CPU score of 28914.01. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 1273.15 GB/sec, dropping to 133.42 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, indicating typical memory architecture performance for its class.

For specialized workloads, the instance demonstrates strong performance in text generation for small language models, reaching 286.19 tokens per second, though performance scales down to 13.32 tokens per second for medium 7B models. Database operations, measured via PassMark, reached 10778.36, while Redis SET operations achieved 23.6 million operations per second. This hardware profile is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, including web serving, software compilation, and moderate database operations, offering a balanced resource density without the added cost of local storage or GPU accelerators.

The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, but it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps. Benchmark results show average performance across standard compute, memory, and database operations, with a PassMark CPU score of 28914.01 and a Redis SET throughput of 23.6 million operations per second. It demonstrates strong capabilities in small-model text generation, making it suitable for general-purpose workloads, web serving, software compilation, and moderate database tasks.

96.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB RAM-to-core ratio.
